So the `data-disabled="true"` attribute on disabled items is persisting because our explicit binding overrides Reka's empty string, which means the filter in `useComboboxArrowKeyLoop.getEnabledOptions` that checks `el.dataset.disabled !== ''` will actually keep those disabled options in the list since the value is `'true'` rather than empty.

This causes wrap-around navigation to potentially highlight disabled options, which then visually disappear due to the CSS rule that only shows highlighting on non-disabled items, leaving the keyboard navigation feeling stuck. The real issue is that the explicit `:data-disabled` binding in ComboboxItem.vue conflicts with what the filter expects — either we need to remove the redundant binding and let Reka handle it, or align the filter logic to match our explicit binding approach.

I've confirmed that the fallthrough `data-disabled="true"` takes precedence over reka's empty string. Now I'll check if `changeHighlight` guards against disabled elements and review the remaining files.

I'm realizing that Reka's own `getCollectionItem()` method also filters out disabled items using the same `dataset.disabled !== ""` check, so when ComboboxItem.vue renders `:data-disabled="props.disabled || undefined"` as the string "true", it breaks both our custom navigation loop and Reka's internal disabled-skip logic—disabled items now have `dataset.disabled === 'true'` which passes the filter instead of being skipped.

This means keyboard navigation can highlight disabled items, which is an accessibility violation. Selection might still be guarded at the click handler level through ListboxItem's `handleSelect` check, but the highlight/focus behavior on disabled options breaks expected a11y patterns. I need to verify whether the data-disabled attribute merge actually affects the same DOM element in our ComboboxItem template.

Looking at the component hierarchy: our ComboboxItem passes `data-disabled` through its attributes, which flow onto the ListboxItem vnode, then merge into the Primitive's root element with fallthrough winning — so "true" overwrites reka's empty string. The existing test passes with "true", but there's no test verifying that keyboard navigation actually skips disabled items. The fix is to remove the `data-disabled` binding from ComboboxItem entirely since reka already handles it correctly.

I need to verify whether ComboboxItem uses `inheritAttrs: false` and selectively forwards attributes — if so, our fallthrough might get dropped entirely, which would explain why the test still sees the attribute.

Reka's ComboboxItem inherits attributes by default, so our fallthrough `data-disabled: true` gets applied to the ListboxItem root and overrides its own empty `data-disabled` attribute — I confirmed this with an SSR test showing the rendered value as `"true"`. There's also an edge case where Reka throws an error if `props.value` is an empty string, and since ComboboxItem passes `:value="props.value ?? ''"`, undefined values get converted to empty strings, which could trigger that error.

The groups mapping in Combobox.vue sets `value: get(item, valueKey) ?? ''` for object items, so if an item is missing the valueKey, it gets an empty string value and crashes when rendering N8nComboboxItem. This is a misconfiguration issue with medium confidence (~75%) — the `?? ''` fallback explicitly creates the crash condition, though it only happens with malformed items or empty string items in the array. Label and separator type items also go through this mapping but don't render N8nComboboxItem since the template branches on type, so they're safe.

The typed API requires `value` to be present, so crashes only occur in untyped or dynamic misuse — below the reporting threshold. But I've found the real issue: reka's `getCollectionItem()` filters disabled items using `dataset.disabled !== ''`, while our explicit `:data-disabled="props.disabled || undefined"` renders "true" instead, which overrides reka's empty string check and causes disabled items to be treated as enabled in keyboard navigation.

On the cleanup side, I'm verifying that the `useComboboxArrowKeyLoop` watcher properly removes its capture-phase listener when the component unmounts — Vue does invoke onCleanup callbacks when the watcher stops, so that's handled correctly. ComboboxArrowKeyLoop rendering as a comment node is expected since it's a script-only component with no template.

For TagsInput in multiple mode, the enter key is prevented to avoid creating tags since the combobox handles selection. When tags are removed, the full array gets emitted via update:modelValue. Single-mode object values work correctly too — the label resolves via labelKey and hasValue checks properly for undefined/null/empty strings. There's a minor edge case where object identity comparison could fail if a consumer passes a different instance with the same value, but that's acceptable to skip.

Now I need to clarify the false-positive test issue: since HEAD intentionally set openOnFocus to always true, the test asserting the combobox stays closed when openOnFocus is false now contradicts the actual behavior and only passes because of that override.

The critical issues to fix are the unused typecheck errors in Combobox.vue (attrs and getCurrentInstance imports), and the openOnFocus API which is dead code but still exposed in the type definitions, documented, and tested with a false assertion. I need to either remove the prop entirely or properly forward it to the popover component.

For the important fixes: DropdownMenuItem has a visual regression where the floating-item mixin is overriding font-size and line-height, so I'll need to either parameterize the mixin or reset those values after including it. There's also a duplicate `@forward 'input';` statement in the mixins index that needs removing.

On the side notes: linting isn't runnable due to a broken eslint plugin link, there's a test false positive pattern I've already covered, the redundant `:data-disabled` on ComboboxItem is harmless, and the `value ?? ''` pattern could trigger issues with reka if items are missing the valueKey. I should verify the line numbers for issue 4 in the final file.

not caused by this diff).

  works — disabled options empirically render `data-disabled=""`, matching the reka convention.
Wrap-around tests exist and pass. Sizes extracted into `_input.scss` mixins are value-identical to
the old inline CSS in `Input.vue`. TagsInput `embedded` mode is wired coherently. Test coverage for
the Combobox itself is broad (41 tests: rendering, item types, filtering, clearable, v-model
single/multiple, events, slots).

### Critical (90–100)

**1. Typecheck fails — unused `attrs` and `instance` (confidence 100)**
`packages/frontend/@n8n/design-system/src/v2/components/Combobox/Combobox.vue:59-60`
```
error TS6133: 'attrs' is declared but its value is never read.
error TS6133: 'instance' is declared but its value is never read.
```
Explicit AGENTS.md violation ("Typecheck is critical before committing") and it breaks CI.
**Fix:** delete both lines and the now-unused `useAttrs` / `getCurrentInstance` imports (template
uses `$attrs` directly, which doesn't need `useAttrs`).

**2. `openOnFocus` prop is dead API guarded by a false-positive test (confidence 92)**
- `Combobox.vue:230` hardcodes `:open-on-focus="true"`; `props.openOnFocus` is never read (not in
  the `reactivePick` list at line 65).
- `Combobox.types.ts:12-14` deliberately `Omit`s reka's `openOnFocus` and then re-adds
  `openOnFocus?: boolean` as a public prop.
- `component-combobox.md:54` documents it: "Open dropdown when input receives focus | default:
  `true`".
- `Combobox.test.ts:477` ("should stay closed on focus when openOnFocus is false") passes only
  because `waitFor`'s first synchronous check runs before Vue flushes the open state. **Empirically
confirmed:** with `openOnFocus: false`, the popover *does* open after flush.

The HEAD commit ("make openOnFocus always true") suggests hardcoding is intentional, but the prop,
doc line, and test were left behind — the public API and docs now lie, and the test asserts
behavior that doesn't exist.
**Fix (pick one, per the intent):** (a) remove `openOnFocus` from `ComboboxProps`, the md doc line,
and the false-positive test; or (b) forward it: `:open-on-focus="props.openOnFocus ?? true"` and
rewrite the test to assert after a flush (e.g. `await nextTick()`/timeout before the closed
assertion).

### Important (80–89)

**3. Silent visual regression in N8nDropdownMenu items (confidence 85)**
`packages/frontend/@n8n/design-system/src/components/N8nDropdownMenu/DropdownMenuItem.vue:395` +
`src/css/mixins/_floating-item.scss:8-9`
`.item` previously had `font-size: var(--font-size--2xs)` (12px) and `line-height: 1`; the shared
`floating-item` mixin now applies `--font-size--sm` (14px) and `--line-height--sm` (1.25).
`.item-label` inherits, so every dropdown menu item across the app grows two size steps. The commit
("Removed sizes from combobox items") only mentions combobox — nothing indicates this
cross-component change was intended, and it's outside the branch's stated scope (DS-289 combobox).
**Fix:** parameterize the mixin (`@mixin floating-item($font-size: var(--font-size--sm),
$line-height: var(--line-height--sm))`) and pass `--font-size--2xs`/`1` from
`DropdownMenuItem.vue`, or re-declare `font-size`/`line-height` after the `@include`. If the size
unification *is* intended, it should be stated in the PR and visually reviewed.

**4. Duplicate `@forward 'input';` (confidence 85)**
`packages/frontend/@n8n/design-system/src/css/mixins/index.scss:6` and `:8` — the diff adds a
second `@forward 'input';` (alphabetical insert) without removing the pre-existing one two lines
below. Harmless in Dart Sass but clear diff noise/slop.
**Fix:** delete line 8.

### Notes (below threshold, no action required)

- `Combobox.vue:62` — `const slots = defineSlots<...>()` return value is also unused (template uses
  `$slots`); vue-tsc doesn't flag it, but drop the assignment while fixing issue 1.
- `ComboboxItem.vue:23` — `:data-disabled="props.disabled || undefined"` is redundant (reka's
  `ListboxItem` already emits `data-disabled=""`); empirically harmless today, but if it ever won
the merge it would break reka's disabled-skip navigation (which filters on `dataset.disabled !==
''`). Safer to remove.
- `Combobox.vue:122` — `value: get(item, props.valueKey) ?? ''` maps a missing value to `''`, which
  reka's `ComboboxItem` rejects with a thrown Error; only reachable through untyped/misconfigured
items.
- The `ComboboxTrigger` icon button inherits reka's untranslated default `aria-label="Show popup"`.
- Lint must be verified once the workspace link for `@n8n/eslint-plugin-design-system` is repaired
  — I could not rule out lint findings.

subagents; the parent session owns that fanout. This report is the completed second-pass review.
